description: Climbing Cone Ridge - Where Are We?

tokens: I'm going to head on up the hill to (Station) B.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And we still need an EMU check from Ed.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Either still bagging the sample or taking his "after" photos) Okay, Fred.
I'll give it to you in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Can you catch up with me all right?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Al's heading up with the MET.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, Houston, I'm trailing along behind Al now.
I'm starting to catch up with him.
And - it hasn't been described for you before - the MET tracks make a very smooth pattern in the surface, reminiscent of driving a tractor through a plowed field.
It smoothes it out and makes a very smooth, distinct pattern, and probably, oh, a quarter of an inch deep, no more.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It leaves gaps every now and then as it bounces.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It's this big crater over here, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It (Station B)'s way up the hill.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think it's up the hill.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: B is the crater we go...This is the crater we go by on the way to B.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'm looking for a contact somewhere in here, but it's not apparent at this point.
Surface texture seems to be very much the same; from the standpoint of soil-bearing properties, it's still about the same softness, and it still has the same raindrop pattern.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Oh, Fredo; you wanted a EMU check from me.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And continuing the description a little bit, Houston.
Trying to think of an adequate description or comparison to something we've already seen, but I don't think there is one.
Incidentally, I see a string of craters down to the south - (correcting himself) a string of boulders - to the south of us that may prove to be a ray pattern from Cone.
And I observe, as we get closer to Cone, the number of large boulders is increasing.
We're going to go past some here in a couple of minutes near about a 20-foot-wide, fairly fresh crater.
The boulders - a dozen of them or so - are 4 or 5 feet in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let's see if we can find us...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: This crater is the one, I think, Al.
It's halfway between A and B, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Can you see the boulders off to the side there on the map?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, they don't show very well.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You should be able to spot that little chain of craters just to the south of us on the map, if that's where we think we are.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Boy, that little chain of craters right there...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: That will make us right here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: There's no big one to go with it.
No sharp one to go with it.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Probably on the move again) That's probably Weird right up there.
We're probably about even with Weird right now, although you can't see it on the ridge.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: That's Weird, that big one right over there, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I think B is that deep crater right directly ahead of us, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think...See that crater right over there that we came by?
To the south, the big one?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I think this is the crater that...that's B.
I think this boulder field, we can see it here if we look.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) We have to be considerably past Weird.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Not even halfway to the rim of Cone yet.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Yeah, I don't think you have to worry too much about the exact position of site B.
It appears you're getting close to the general area, and that should be good enough on B.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I think we're very close to it.
I think this crater we just went by is probably it, but it's very hard to tell, Fredo.
I don't see anything else that might be it, unless it's the next crater up.
That next crater up is this one right here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That crater is that crater right up there.
That crater is the crater over to the left of it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Where do you think B is?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think B's the one we just passed; back there where we were talking.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And here's the little...Ah hah, it is!
Here's the little double crater right beside it.
See, there's that crater; see, there's the little double crater; it's right there in front of you.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Al and Ed, this is a grab sample at B, and we need the panorama.
And while somebody's doing that, we can get a site description.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And while Al takes the pan, I'll go ahead and give you a site description.
The area here is in an area of considerably more boulders, a larger boulder field, more numerous boulders than we've seen in the past.
We've just come into it as we approached B from A.
Now there were boulders to the north of us; we previously talked of boulders to the north, and doggone it, they may turn out to be a ray pattern.
However, where we are now, we're about on the edge of a general boulder population lining the flank of Cone Crater.
Now they're not too numerous at this point.
There's a lot of them buried, half buried, a few of the smaller ones sitting on the surface.
These boulders are filleted, and we'll have to sample that filleting later.
The surface texture - the fine - appears very much the same as what we've been walking on all along.
And about the only difference we could see is probably a larger number of smaller craters.
I say 'probably'; they're so numerous that unless you really make a population count, you can't tell.
I'm guessing a larger number of craters - probably secondaries from Cone perhaps - and certainly a larger number of boulders lying around.
Now, most of these boulders are rounded.
There are a few angular ones.
There are a few rocks with angularities; but, by and large, you can see edges that have been chipped off indicating the beginning of a smoothing process.
And some of them are far beyond the beginning of smoothing; they're worn down pretty well.
And most of the rough edges are where they have fractured and perhaps turned over.
Most of them appear to be along fractures of where other rocks are sitting near them that might have once been a part of that boulder.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And has Al got the grab sample completed and up?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I'm just going to give you a quick stereo on it.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Fredo, we expect that you're (means "we're") going to fall behind here; there's no way we can help it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Grab sample from the west rim of Bravo Crater, bag 5 November.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Now, Fredo, to complete this description.
We are standing on a fairly high point...Well, not really on a high point, about halfway up the slope.
To our north and slightly to the west of us seems to be the low point in this area.
It's surrounded by a rim that's reminiscent of a very, very old crater.
The topography doesn't show up on the map, but it, indeed, is there.
About 500 yards to the north and west is the lowest point that I can see in this area.
Okay, we ready to press on?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, as soon as I get my handle screwed back on here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, the next stop is the top of Cone.
Let's get everything secured for that trip.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You've got a frame count of 34 from Al.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You want to go first and I'll follow.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: To the top of Cone Crater.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll have to go almost to the east here, and then on up by Flank.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: East and a little to the...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: [Al may be looking at the crater at CS.3/78.4 (
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Uh...Yeah, right up...I can just barely see the rim of it on the far side of it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Right, so we probably ought to head directly for Flank and on up from there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Houston, as we go across here, this ground is - Al's probably previously described it - but it's very undulating.
I would suspect that there is not 10 yards at the most between what were once old craters.
They are most of them worn down, but the surface is continuously undulating.
There's hardly a level spot anywhere.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Lots of...As we come on up toward Cone, we're getting to see lots more buried rocks, bigger rocks.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're keeping our eyes open for a contact here.
But I guess the Sun angle makes it very difficult to see.
However, I expect that by the time we get a little closer up to Flank (Crater)...Let me pull it for a while.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: By the time we get a little closer up to Flank (Crater), we might find some kind of a contact.
(Pause) The ridge of Cone Crater to the north is very apparent, as we expected that it would be.
It stretches off into the distance and meets with the far horizon.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Fredo, I'm trying to find something distinctive to say about some of these craters we're going by, and it's very hard to do so.
They're all smooth-walled except the very freshest ones; and we're coming by a very fresh one now, which is rubblely on the in(side)...Hey!
It may even...That has some pretty good chunks of rubble on the inside.
This is about the freshest crater this size we've seen, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: This is a very fresh crater.
It's about (garbled)- it's about opposite to the crater at stop E.
It's a crater about 20 meters in diameter and about 2 meters deep, and I'll get a quick (pause) rock from the side.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Al just dropped down on a knee to pick up a rock, and he (meaning his knee) went in 3 or 4 inches (into the soft soil).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Come on, give me your hand.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Wait a minute, I got it now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: That's just a quick hand sample from the side of that crater.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Do you think you're following us and know about where we are, Fredo?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: ...I think, has reading for you just past the position abeam of (Station) E.
(Pause) Looking about halfway between D and B...
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Responding to 'abeam of E') Yeah, that's good...
(Listens to the second half of Haise's remark) Rog.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Climb's fairly gentle at this point but it's definitely uphill.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And that sample from the west rim of the crater, which we described as blocky is in bag 6.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: The going is still very smooth as far as the area that we're able to pick out.
Of course, we're tracing a kind of sinuous course here, staying out of the craters.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, Fredo, to help further locate us, if you can, we're going by two very...Well, (two) fairly fresh craters.
I don't think quite as fresh as the one we were just talking about.
The eastmost one is fresher than...(Correcting himself) The westmost one is the freshest.
They're separated about 75 to 100 feet, and they're about 25 to 30 feet across and 5 or 6 feet deep; 5 feet deep, I guess.
The westmost one has got small blocks in it.
The eastmost one is very smooth.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, you described the blocks there a couple of times.
Now, I think you used the term "rubble".
By that I assume you implied they were just lying loose (with) nothing really in place.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I'm not sure that's quite true, Fred.
Some of it looked like stuff that belonged there, that had not fallen there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: There's a lot of glass in that rock, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It looked like some of that so-called rubble looked like it might be the residual of an impact just lying in the bottom.
And, Houston, we're passing a rock much too big to pick up.
There's a whale of a lot of glass in it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Garbled) like it was splattered with glass.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It looks...(Listens) It's about foot and a half, 2-footer...Yeah, about a foot and a half across.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That was a glass splatter, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, Al and Ed, why don't we take a little rest here for a minute, and we'd like another camera count, too.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We haven't taken any pictures since the last ones, I don't think.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll slow down the traverse here.
Should be Flank right here, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Should be Flank right over here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Just out of sight, you mean.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I assume you're on the move now and heading toward Flank.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Heading toward where we think Flank is.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'll pull for a while, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I got it for a while.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Why don't we pull up beside this big crater.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Take a break, get the map, and see if we can find out exactly where we are.
This one should be distinctive enough.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: While you're stopped here, we could use a photo pan.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Pause) If you'll take the pan, Al, I'll grab the map and get over here and see if we can find...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let me pull it up on a little more level ground.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: That old LM looks like it's got a flat (tire) over there, the way it's leaning.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Ed and Al, we're going to have a site handover here.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: The pan is complete on magazine (pause) magazine Lima-Lima.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Start on up toward the rim?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (To Al) All I'm getting is the feedback on my own voice.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Do you want me to pull awhile, Al?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) I can't really spot this crater (on the map), but I think I know where we are.
We're pretty close to where you said we were.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Houston, your transmissions are still unreadable.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: How do you read me now?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I think it's dead ahead of you, Al.
This is probably it, right here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let's just double check and see.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It's got about a 4-meter-radius crater in the south wall.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We're going by Flank on the way up.
We're passing to the north side of it.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Well, we still have a little way to go.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm just putting the map away.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Evidently, that station switch gave us some problems.
I've been copying both of you all the way though.
We have you now just passing Flank.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We've been copying you most of the time, and I have you by Flank now.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And the grade is getting pretty steep.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Breathing more heavily) And the soil here is a bit firmer, I think, than we've been on before.
Except around the mounds in between craters where it's been thrown out.
But, by and large, it seems to have a little firmer footing.
We're not sinking in as deep.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That should help you with the climb there.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Al's picked up the...Al's got the back of the MET now, and we're carrying it up.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: There are two guys here (Cernan and Engle) that figured you'd carry it up.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Said there's two guys sitting next to me here that kind of figured you'd end up carrying it up.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Well, it'll roll along here, except we just move faster carrying it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You want to rest here by this rock?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: This is the first big boulder we've seen, Houston.
I think it's worthwhile taking a picture of it with the close-up.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We probably ought to take a pan to locate everything here, while you're taking a close-up (with the Gold Camera).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You're shooting a close-up shot of a big boulder.
(Pause) About what's the size of this one, Al?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: The shot's been taken on the close-up counter number 317.
This particular one is only about 12-feet long by about 4-feet wide.
There are some evidences of some crystal shining through some of the fractures.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And I've taken a Hasselblad of the rock and will take a pan now from this location.
Help document our course going to the top of Cone Crater.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Probably taking a pan frame toward the south) And I can look right across into the breach in the north rim of
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Not where I took the picture (with the close-up stereo camera), but some fillets around the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I believe that was; if I remember it.
And I'm going to move on out.
Al's ahead of me here (with the MET).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're starting up the last flank of the crater (means the Cone Crater Ridge) now, Houston.
The slope is probably about, oh, 18 percent.
The surface texture is still pretty much the same as far as the raindrop pattern is concerned.
But we seem to find an increasing population of smaller rocks.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Breathing heavily, again) There's small rocks and smaller, fresher craters, as well.
Well, wait a minute, maybe I'm being deceived.
With this slope, the Sun angle is entirely different than it is on the flat land.
The craters look sharper in these shadows.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'd like to stop and rest here for a minute, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Boy, I tell you, we're really going to get a panorama.
We've got a tremendous one here, Houston, already.
And we're not quite to the rim.
Back towards out (may have meant to say 'out towards')
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I thought we were in a low spot with the LM, but it turns out we're really not in the lowest spot around, I don't think.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'd say it's probably the lowest spot right...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Oh, right in that particular local area.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: But that's the lowest spot over to the right (north) that I was talking about.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, there's a crater over there, it's true...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Doggone it, you can sure be deceived by slopes here.
The Sun angle is very deceiving.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, let me pull a while.
(They trade off) You ready to go?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, let me go back to Min cool...Minimum cool first.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I guess right straight up is the best way to go.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Right straight up is the best way to go.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And the boulder fields that Al pointed out, the rocks and boulders are getting more numerous toward the top here.
However, it's nothing like the rubble and the large boulders that we saw at the Nevada Test Site.
Now, this is surprising to me.
I expected it to be more like that.
But it is not, at least not where we're looking now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, we haven't reached the rim (of Cone), yet.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Oh boy, we got fooled on that one.
I'm not sure that was Flank we were at a minute ago, either.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That's the...That's the east (ridge)...(That's the) little shoulder running down from the Cone.
We're going to hit it (perhaps meaning Flank Crater) on the south side.
We'll have to move on around of it.
This looks like easy going right here.
See, there's the boulder field that shows in the photograph; it's right up ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: There's the crater (meaning Cone) up there, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: They'd like you to take another stop here.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We're really going up a pretty steep slope here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We kind of figured that from listening to you.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Well, now, that's apparently the rim of Cone over there.
And we're about...almost 2 hours (into the EVA) now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We're showing 1:57 and a half now, Al.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Pause) That's at least 30 minutes up there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And I would say we'd probably do better to go up to some of those boulders there; document that (and) use that as the turnaround point.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It's going to take longer than we expected.
Our positions are all in doubt now, Fredo.
What we were looking at was a flank, but it wasn't really...The top of it wasn't the rim of Cone.
We've got a ways to go yet.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, perhaps you can think with us if you want.
I'd say that the rim is at least 30 minutes away.
We're approaching the edge of the boulder field here on the south flank.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And what I'm proposing is perhaps we use that as the turnaround point.
It seems to me that we spend a lot more time in traverse if we don't, and we don't get very many samples.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, just a couple of questions they have up now.
They'd like you to note if you do see any dust, particularly on the top surfaces of boulders in the area.
And, any comparisons between the boulders you see distributed around.
Are they all the same or do some types appear different?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: It's too early to make that sort of judgment, but we'll tell you when we get there.
We're not really in that boulder territory yet.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I think, Fredo, if you'll keep those questions in mind, the best thing for us to do is to get up here and document samples of what I feel is pretty sure Cone ejecta.
And then, when we head down-Sun, we'll be able to see these subtle variations and rock types a lot better than we are right now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Well, let's head for these two babies up here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'd...(Pause) No, let's keep going around this crater (probably Flank), but...(Pause) Think that's right here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I thought we'd get those boulders up there, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Let's head right for that boulder field at the top.
I think we'll be where we want to be.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, right...Clear on up at the top, you mean.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I don't think we'll have time to go up there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Oh, let's give it a whirl.
We can't stop without looking into Cone Crater.
(Garbled) everything if we don't get there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I think we'll waste an awful lot of time traveling and not much documenting.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Well, the information we're going to find, I think, is going to be right on top.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Look at this; you're going through...(We) just kicked up a layer of some very light gray fine underneath the...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: As you look back along your path, there's quite a bit of it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Get out of this crater...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Fredo, how far behind our timeline are we?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: As best I can tell right now (pause) about 25 minutes down now.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We'll be an hour down by the time we get to the top of that thing.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Well, I think we're going to find what we're looking for up there.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: In view of your assay of where your location is and how long it's going to take to get to Cone, the word from the Backroom is they'd like you to consider where you are the edge of Cone Crater.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That decision, I guess, was based on Al's estimate of another, at least, 30 minutes and, of course, we cannot see that from here.
It's kind of your judgment on that.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Pause) Why don't we lose our bet, Al, and leave the MET and get on up there?
We could make it a lot faster without it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well...I think what we're looking at right here in this boulder field, Ed, is the stuff that's ejected from Cone.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: But not the lowermost part, which is what we're interested in.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll press on a little farther, Houston.
And keep your eye on the time.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, as of right now, we have a 30-minute extension.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: And Al, did you copy 30-minute extension?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Get up at this little rise here and take a panorama.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Probably having stopped) Okay, Al's going to Medium flow.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And I'll take a pan from here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, I'll tell you, it's a fantastic view from here.
(Pause) (Breathing heavily) As this pan will show.
(Long Pause) We're approaching the edge of the rugged boulder field to the west rim.
It appears as though the best for us to do will be go to the west rim and document from there even though the Sun angle may not be quite as good.
Well, we're pushing on in that direction.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Confused by Al's statement) You're moving to the west then.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Al is back to Min flow, and we're moving again.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, Al and Ed, Deke says he'll cover the bet if you'll drop the MET.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Well, it's not that hard with the MET.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: No, the MET's not slowing us down, Houston.
It's just a question of time.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You caught a boulder with your wheel when you went around that corner.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Just bear a little more left.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're now right in middle of the boulder field on the west rim.
We haven't quite reached the rim yet.
Want to rest here a minute?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Breathing very heavily; speech labored) Let's take a look at the map.
I think we're closer than that.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'll just go ahead slowly with this.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Pause) Let's see if we can spot this one, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Al, it looks like you'd be a little more comfortable there if you're on Intermediate.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: That crater right there (on the lunar surface in front of them) is that one right there (on the map).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) Want to pull for a while?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We're about the maximum elevation now, Houston.
It's leveled out a little bit.
And it looks like we'll be approaching the rim here very shortly.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And you can leave the dial in Intermediate.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: If we're in that good a shape, let me set mine, Houston, if I'm okay, too.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I guess the low item is the battery.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Long Pause) Fantastic stabilization; Al, it's going to turn over.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I don't see the crater yet.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: See this boulder pattern and all that we're in here right now?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I thought it (meaning the boulder field) was on the south rim.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Al and Ed, do you have the rim in sight at this time?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I meant the rim of Cone Crater.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: This big boulder right here (on the traverse map), Al, which stands out bigger than anything else (undoubtedly Saddle Rock) ought...We ought to be able to see it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, I don't know what the rim is still way up here from the looks of things.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, Ed and Al, we've already eaten in our 30-minute extension and we're past that now.
I think we'd better proceed with the sampling and continue with the EVA.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We'll start with a pan from here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard
